what_it_does: Defines the Company-wide address grammar as a set of URI schemes, a small Pydantic Provenance record, and canonical fail-loud parsers/canonicalizers for session, composition-step, clone, decision, and image addresses, plus a general @v<n> version-axis helper.
observations: SCHEMES enumerates 21 schemes (run, cas, blob, vec, ui, code, skill, context, guide, session, cap, board, clone, mind, exchange, file, project, vi-vision, decision, image, extraction).
Provenance is the only record shape: address, content_hash, type, produced_by, inputs, agent, created_at, schema_ver.
Most schemes are registered as grammar labels only; their resolution is deferred to other runtime modules.
parse_decision_address supports global/project/user/session frames and normalizes a bare id to global; decision_address always emits the explicit frame.
split_version and versioned_address introduce a cross-scheme @v<n> ordinal version suffix.
parse_image_address requires a channel and a hierarchical in-channel path, and also accepts the @v<n> suffix.
The file contains extensive docstring commentary explaining design rationale, cross-module wiring, and historical commit references.
mind:// and extraction:// appear in SCHEMES but have no parser or explanatory note in this file.
standouts: The file acts as the single grammar home for many address families, with fail-loud parsing.
Decision and image addresses get both parser and canonicalizer, plus the general version suffix convention.
Provenance is the only concrete record shape; most scheme additions are additive labels.
conventions: Fail-loud validation via ValueError for malformed addresses rather than silent fallback.
Canonical parsing declared once in contracts/address.py and consumed by resolvers/gates elsewhere.
Additive scheme registration without bumping schema_ver when only widening the legal scheme set.
Pydantic BaseModel with Field default_factory for list fields.
concerns: SCHEMES includes mind and extraction with no parser, note, or grammar in this file, leaving a gap.
The module-level docstring is very long and mixes rationale, wiring notes, and commit references, increasing drift risk.
parse_image_address strips and filters empty segments, which could collapse double slashes after the channel check.
No tests or examples are present to verify parser edge cases.
Provenance.schema_ver is hardcoded to 1 despite the stated policy of bumping on schema change.
notes: This file is the central address-grammar contract: it declares what addresses look like and how to parse them, while leaving resolution of most label-only schemes to other runtime modules.
questions: What is the grammar and resolver for mind:// and extraction:// schemes?
Is a canonical parser planned for guide://, or is it purely a registry label?
How does the general @v<n> version axis interact with run://'s #run=<id> instance pointer in practice?
What populates and validates the Provenance.type field against registered types (C2/S4)?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is addresses + provenance (Pydantic). The file does that and has grown into a broad grammar registry with parsers for several scheme families; it matches, though it now also carries extensive deferred-scheme commentary.
apparent_themes: One addressed state / unified address space.
Additive evolution without breaking existing record shapes.
Hierarchical, framed addressing for decisions and images.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to make the address grammar the single source of truth for the whole system, so any component can refer to any artifact with a stable, parseable, versionable URI, while keeping actual resolution logic distributed across specialized registries.
novelty: The cross-scheme @v<n> version suffix and the decision:// frame normalization with separate pending registry row versus resolved state from marks are relatively unusual; so is the extensive scheme-label registry that defers resolution rather than implementing it.
contribution: Provides a shared vocabulary and canonical parsers for the system's address space, plus a minimal provenance record shape, so other modules can agree on how addresses are structured and validated without each inventing its own parser.
summary_for_embedding: A Pydantic-based contract module that defines the Company's address grammar, a Provenance record, and canonical fail-loud parsers and canonicalizers for run, session, composition-step, clone, decision, and image addresses plus a general @v<n> version axis.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize the syntax and validation of all Company artifact addresses so that resolvers, gates, and provenance records share one grammar.

symbols.does: Pydantic model for a provenance record tying an artifact address to its content hash, producer, inputs, and metadata.
symbols.description: Provenance stores the run:// address an artifact was written to, the cas:// content hash for integrity, the type, the producing run-address, input lineage addresses, agent, creation time, and a schema version. It is the only record shape in this file.

symbols.does: Builds a run:// mutable pointer string from graph, node, branch, and optional run id.
symbols.description: Constructs the canonical run://<domain>/<intent>/<node>@<branch> base address and appends #run=<id> when a run_id is supplied, representing a mutable pointer to a computation run.

symbols.does: Returns the scheme prefix of an address if it matches a known scheme, else None.
symbols.description: Iterates the SCHEMES tuple and returns the scheme string when the address starts with scheme://; returns None for unknown or malformed prefixes.

symbols.does: Predicate that checks whether an address is a cas:// content address.
symbols.description: Simple boolean helper returning True for addresses beginning with 'cas://', used to identify immutable content-addressed hashes.

symbols.does: Parses session://<sid> and session://<sid>/step/<tool_use_id> addresses, failing loudly on malformed input.
symbols.description: Validates the session:// prefix, splits the session id from an optional /step/ sub-path, and returns a dict with sid and step. It rejects empty ids and any sub-path other than step.

symbols.does: Returns True if the address is a session step form session://<sid>/step/<tool_use_id>.
symbols.description: Wraps parse_session_address and returns whether the step component is non-None, giving a single shape-check shared by resolver and gate logic.

symbols.does: Parses run://<turn>/<member> and run://<turn>/<member>/<index> composition-leg addresses.
symbols.description: Splits the run:// body into turn, member, and optional integer index, returning a dict. It rejects empty segments and non-integer indexes, providing the canonical parser for composition gate targets.

symbols.does: Returns True if the address matches a run_composition leg address shape.
symbols.description: Wraps parse_composition_step_address to give a boolean gate-target check, accepting either a source leg or a downstream indexed leg.

symbols.does: Parses clone://<source-sid>/<cut> addresses into source session id and cut point.
symbols.description: Validates the clone:// prefix and splits on the first slash, returning source_sid and cut. It requires both segments to be non-empty and rejects malformed clone addresses.

symbols.does: Parses decision://<frame>/<id> and bare decision://<id> addresses into frame, scope, and id.
symbols.description: Supports global, project/<id>, user/<id>, and session/<id> frames, treating a bare id as global. It returns a normalized frame string and rejects malformed paths or missing scope ids.

symbols.does: Canonicalizes a parsed decision address back to decision://<frame>/<id> with explicit frame.
symbols.description: Ensures the global frame is always written explicitly, producing the single mark key used by both decision takes and the resolver so bare and global forms share one identity.

symbols.does: Splits a general @v<n> version suffix off any address string.
symbols.description: Uses a regex to detect an ordinal version suffix at the end of an address and returns the base address and version integer; it fails loudly on malformed suffixes like @vfoo.

symbols.does: Builds the canonical versioned address form <base>@v<n>.
symbols.description: Concatenates a base address with an integer version using the @v<n> convention, implementing the cross-scheme content-version axis.

symbols.does: Parses image://<channel>/<path> hierarchical image addresses, including an optional version suffix.
symbols.description: Strips any @v<n> suffix, requires a channel segment, splits the remaining nested path, and returns channel, path, segments, leaf name, leaf flag, and version. It rejects bare image:// addresses.

symbols.does: Builds the canonical image://<channel>/<path> identity string.
symbols.description: Validates that both channel and path are non-empty, strips leading/trailing slashes, and returns the canonical hierarchical image address.
